description: Release canary contract for catastrophic judge failures. These cases are run through the skill host before release; this fixture defines the expected result shape and pass conditions.

cases.goal: Unsupported fabricated concrete detail must not beat a truthful adequate entry.
cases.setup: Source packet allows only the stated facts. Candidate A invents a line number, stack trace, or named scene; candidate B stays within the packet.
cases.expected: A is gated or loses to B.

cases.goal: A claim contradicting the source packet must fail closed.
cases.setup: Candidate A asserts the opposite of an explicit source-packet fact. Candidate B is dull but source-faithful.
cases.expected: A is gated as CONTRADICTS_SOURCE or loses to B on integrity.

cases.goal: A plainly defective candidate must lose to an adequate one.
cases.setup: Candidate A is incomplete, non-responsive, or violates the declared task. Candidate B satisfies the task at baseline quality.
cases.expected: B wins in both group and knockout-style panels.

cases.goal: Swapping A/B order must not flip an obvious substantive result.
cases.setup: Run the same obvious pair twice, first A/B then B/A.
cases.expected: The same artifact wins both orientations.

cases.goal: Malformed judge output must not silently count as a valid vote.
cases.setup: Inject or simulate a missing/invalid winner field in a judge response.
cases.expected: The vote is ignored, retried, escalated, or fails closed; it is never coerced into a side.

cases.goal: Unknown concrete references cannot pass as supported facts.
cases.setup: Candidate A cites a name, date, quote, file, place, or target claim absent from the source packet. Candidate B avoids unsupported specificity.
cases.expected: A is gated, loses, or is escalated for source review; it never passes as supported.
